title: Minty Brazilian Beans Casserole
description: A tangy, shaggy casserole that blends black beans with fresh mint, cilantro, and citrus for a crowd-pleasing Brazilian twist. The dish is satvik-friendly and baked to perfection with a golden crust.
instructions: Soak the black beans overnight in cold water, then drain and rinse.
In a large pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat and sauté the diced onion until translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in the tomato paste, cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper, cooking for another 2 minutes.
Add the soaked beans, water, coconut milk, lemon zest, and citrus juices; bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 60 minutes until beans are tender.
Stir in the chopped cilantro and mint, removing from heat.
Transfer the bean mixture into a greased casserole dish and bake at 180°C for 30 minutes until the top forms a golden crust.
Let the casserole rest for 10 minutes before serving.
